title: ～てまで
titleEn: Express the extent someone goes in order to achieve something; 'to the point of', 'even', 'so far as to'.
explanation: Express the extent someone goes in order to achieve something; 'to the point of', 'even', 'so far as to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てまで grammar point is used to convey the extent someone goes to achieve something, highlighting the action one takes or the sacrifice one makes for a certain purpose. It can be translated as 'to the point of', 'even', or 'so far as to' in English. It is used with verbs, not adjectives or nouns.

title: ～て当然だ
titleEn: Expresses that something is natural or expected.
explanation: Expresses that something is natural or expected. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～て当然だ grammar point is used to express that something is natural, obvious, or expected under the circumstances. It can be translated as 'it's natural that', 'as expected', or 'no wonder' in English.

title: ～というものだ
titleEn: Expressing a general rule or common sense.
explanation: Expressing a general rule or common sense. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～というものだ grammar point is used to emphasize a general rule, a piece of common sense, or an indisputable fact. It often appears in comments or observations about something commonly accepted as true.

title: どうにか～ないものか
titleEn: Express a strong desire or wish for something to be possible.
structure: どうにか + Verb-negative form + ないものか
structureEn: どうにか + Verb-negative form + ないものか
explanation: Express a strong desire or wish for something to be possible. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: どうにか～ないものか is used to express a strong desire or wish for something to be resolved or made possible. It translates roughly as 'isn't there some way...?', 'can't we somehow...?', or 'is there no way...?'

title: ～とおり
titleEn: Express doing something as instructed or describing how something matches a rule or guideline; 'as', 'according to'.
structure: Verb-casual + とおり, い-Adjective + とおり, な-Adjective + だとおり, Noun + だとおり
structureEn: Verb-casual + とおり, い-Adjective + とおり, な-Adjective + だとおり, Noun + だとおり
explanation: Express doing something as instructed or describing how something matches a rule or guideline; 'as', 'according to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とおり grammar point is used to express doing something exactly as instructed or describing how something matches a guideline or is exactly as one heard/expected. It can be translated as 'as', 'according to', or 'the same way...'.

title: ～とか
titleEn: Used to list examples or express uncertainty; 'such as', 'like', 'or something like that'.
structure: Verb-casual + とか, い-Adjective + とか, な-Adjective + だとか, Noun + だとか
structureEn: Verb-casual + とか, い-Adjective + とか, な-Adjective + だとか, Noun + だとか
explanation: Used to list examples or express uncertainty; 'such as', 'like', 'or something like that'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とか grammar point can be used to list examples or express a somewhat vague mention of items/activities. It can be translated as 'such as', 'like', or 'or something like that'.

title: ～ところ
titleEn: Express a specific point in time or state; 'when', 'just about', or 'in the middle of'.
structure: Verb-casual + ところ, い-Adjective + ところ, な-Adjective + なところ, Noun + のところ
structureEn: Verb-casual + ところ, い-Adjective + ところ, な-Adjective + なところ, Noun + のところ
explanation: Express a specific point in time or state; 'when', 'just about', or 'in the middle of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ところ grammar point is used to describe a specific point in time or stage of action. Depending on the verb tense, it can mean 'just about to do something', 'in the middle of doing something', or 'just did something'.

title: ～どころか
titleEn: Express 'far from', 'not just', 'not only', or 'to say nothing of'.
structure: Verb-casual + どころか, い-Adjective + どころか, な-Adjective + どころか, Noun + どころか
structureEn: Verb-casual + どころか, い-Adjective + どころか, な-Adjective + どころか, Noun + どころか
explanation: Express 'far from', 'not just', 'not only', or 'to say nothing of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～どころか grammar point is used to emphasize that reality is far from what one might expect or that something is not only one way, but actually goes beyond it. It can be translated as 'far from', 'not just', or 'to say nothing of'.
